summary: The `pnpm audit` command detected a moderate-severity security vulnerability in the `ip-address` package (XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods, GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g). The vulnerable version (<=10.1.0) is pulled in transitively via `network__fetch > @pnpm/network.agent > @pnpm/network.proxy-agent > socks-proxy-agent > socks > ip-address`. The audit exited with code 1, failing the CI job.
root_cause: `pnpm audit` found 1 moderate vulnerability: `ip-address` package version <=10.1.0 has an XSS flaw (GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g) in Address6 HTML-emitting methods. The patched version is >=10.1.1. This caused `pnpm audit` to exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: │ moderate            │ ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods   │
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific vulnerability detected by pnpm audit
Shows the affected version range of the ip-address package
Shows the transitive dependency path through which the vulnerable package is included
Confirms that pnpm audit found a vulnerability, triggering a non-zero exit
Confirms the audit command failed with exit code 1, causing the CI job to fail

suggested_fix: Update the `ip-address` dependency (directly or via its parent packages in the chain `socks > ip-address`) to version >=10.1.1, or add an override/resolution in `package.json` to force `ip-address` to >=10.1.1.
